name: Jürgen Mantzke
photoAlt: Jürgen Mantzke, UX and product designer
identity: The personal website of Jürgen Mantzke, UX and product designer
brandSummary: Transforming complex domains into clear, direction‑setting product foundations. I combine deep research, systems‑level framing, and AI‑supported exploration to define what a product should be, scale clarity across teams, and turn ambiguity into confident forward motion.
siteCredit: This case study website was vibe-coded with Claude Opus, Figma, and GitHub in Visual Studio Code.
paragraphs: I began my career in editorial design, working across regional and national publications where I art directed photo shoots, collaborated with illustrators, and managed full print production. Those years shaped my eye for clarity and visual storytelling, and my work earned several design awards, appeared in national design annuals, and was featured in the New York Times. That foundation in visual communication continues to guide how I approach digital product design.

As digital platforms became central to how people consumed content, I shifted into UI and UX by teaching myself HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and CMS platforms like WordPress and Drupal. I also worked with Flash, SVG, and motion graphics. Consulting for editorial sites, ecommerce, and web applications gave me hands‑on experience with real constraints and fast problem solving, and it set the foundation for the UX work I do today.

Over the past decade, I have focused on designing clear, data‑driven solutions for complex systems. I have worked with organizations such as Alaska Airlines, Amazon, Boeing, CBRE, Hitachi, Microsoft, and Yale University, learning how to quickly understand new domains and design systems that scale. My recent work centers on design system architecture, workflow clarity, and AI‑supported processes that help teams work more efficiently.

Today, I work as a UX consultant supporting teams that need strong design system practice, clear thinking, and practical guidance on integrating AI into their design and engineering workflows. I partner with organizations that want to simplify complexity and create experiences that feel intuitive and genuinely helpful. My approach is collaborative, hands‑on, and grounded in real‑world problem solving, and I am available for consulting engagements, freelance collaborations, or full‑time roles where thoughtful design can make a meaningful impact.

certifications.name: Stanford University Professional Certificate in AI for UX
certifications.badgeAlt: Stanford University Professional Certificate in AI for UX
